summary: DHH broke his arm playing soccer with other programmers and is frustrated by how much it slows down his typing. Despite 15 years of rollerblading without injury, a casual soccer game among tech workers did the damage. He notes soccer is statistically one of the most dangerous sports in Denmark. He'll be sidelined from programming and blogging for about two weeks.
key_points: DHH broke his arm playing casual soccer with other programmers, dubbed 'nerd ball'
A broken arm reduces typing speed to roughly one-fourth or one-fifth of normal, devastating for a programmer
Soccer is statistically one of the most hazardous sports in Denmark despite seeming casual
He went 15 years rollerblading without serious injury but got hurt immediately playing soccer
money_quotes: Soccer, though, is an inherently dangerous sport.
And does it ever suck to break your arm as a computer programmer.
Typing is sooo slow. It's not even close to being half speed. More like one fourth or fifth.
Yesterday, I broke my arm playing against a whole bunch of other computer professionals. "Nerd ball", we called it.
Oh my, I hate soccer right now...
key_insight: A casual soccer game among programmers proves more dangerous than years of rollerblading, and a broken arm is uniquely punishing for someone whose livelihood depends on typing.
